skill.name: video-edit
skill.description: Route video edit requests to RunComfy models for restyling, background changes, packaging edits, motion transfer, and outfit swaps.
skill.summary: Edit existing videos with intent-based RunComfy model routing.
skill.author: doany-ai

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many Markdown backticks, URLs, placeholder paths, and model names. Review found no prompt injection attempt or confirmed malware, but the skill intentionally runs the RunComfy CLI, sends media URLs and prompts to RunComfy, stores tokens, and downloads generated files.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Execution Required
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to install and run the RunComfy CLI, including model calls with user-supplied JSON. This is expected functionality, but it executes local commands and depends on the installed CLI behavior.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and central to the skill purpose. I found no command obfuscation or hidden shell expansion beyond normal CLI usage.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party Media Processing
medium_findings.description: The skill sends prompts and source media URLs to RunComfy services, then downloads generated output from RunComfy domains. Users should treat submitted media and prompts as shared with a third-party processor.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documentation states that the CLI posts to the Model API and downloads generated RunComfy URLs. This confirms network processing, but not credential exfiltration or unrelated callbacks.

low_findings.title: Local Token Storage Documented
low_findings.description: The skill notes that RunComfy login stores an API token under the user config directory with owner-only permissions. This is normal credential storage, but users should protect the host account and prefer environment tokens in CI.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The token path and mode are documented directly. This is a managed credential location, not evidence of secret theft.

low_findings.title: Static Weak-Crypto and Traversal Hits Dismissed
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography hits map to model names, field names, exit codes, and Markdown examples. The path traversal hits are placeholder URLs and output path placeholders, not file traversal logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line review shows documentation text, placeholder media URLs, and model-routing tables. No cryptographic operation or traversal implementation appears in SKILL.md.

dangerous_patterns.title: Runs runcomfy CLI With User Inputs
dangerous_patterns.description: Command examples pass prompts, video URLs, reference images, and output paths to runcomfy run. This is legitimate for the skill, but it is a real execution boundary.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command patterns are present in executable bash examples. The skill also claims the CLI sends JSON directly, reducing but not removing execution risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: Downloads Generated Files To Local Output Directory
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill explains that the CLI downloads generated RunComfy URLs into the requested output directory. Generated media can consume disk space or contain untrusted content.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The download behavior and size cap are documented. The cap reduces disk-fill risk, but local file writes remain part of normal operation.

content.value_statement: Video edits often fail when the wrong model handles the request. This skill routes each edit to a suitable RunComfy model and supplies focused prompt patterns.
actual_capabilities: Classifies edit intent and selects Wan 2.7, Kling 2.6 Pro, or Lucy Edit Restyle.
Provides RunComfy CLI commands for background swaps, packaging swaps, motion transfer, and outfit changes.
Documents model-specific input fields, duration limits, resolution limits, and audio options.
Suggests prompts that prioritize identity, pose, motion, lip movement, and framing preservation.
Explains RunComfy exit codes, token handling, endpoints, and output download behavior.
limitations: Requires the RunComfy CLI, a RunComfy account, and usable source media URLs.
Each request uses one route; it does not blend multiple models in one call.
Model limits apply, including duration, resolution, aspect ratio, and media size limits.
Submitted prompts and media URLs are processed by RunComfy services.

use_cases.title: Refresh Marketing Videos
use_cases.description: Swap product backgrounds or packaging while preserving camera motion and hand movement.

use_cases.title: Restyle Creator Clips
use_cases.description: Change lighting, outfits, or scene style while keeping identity and motion stable.

use_cases.title: Transfer Character Motion
use_cases.description: Map motion from a reference clip onto a target image or character video.

prompt_templates.title: Simple Background Swap
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a talking-head clip.
prompt_templates.prompt: Edit this video with RunComfy. Preserve the speaker face, pose, lip movement, and audio. Change only the background to a clean office.

prompt_templates.title: Packaging Replacement
prompt_templates.scenario: Use a reference image for a product design change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the video and reference image to replace only the package design. Keep hand movement, framing, lighting, and source audio unchanged.

prompt_templates.title: Outfit Restyle
prompt_templates.prompt: Change the subject outfit to professional business attire. Preserve face, body motion, camera movement, and the original scene composition.

prompt_templates.title: Reference Motion Transfer
prompt_templates.prompt: Transfer the motion from this reference video to the target character. Keep character identity stable and preserve the reference audio when appropriate.

output_examples.input: A user asks to restyle a talking-head video with a modern office background.
output_examples.output: The assistant selects Wan 2.7 Edit-Video, prepares a preservation-first prompt, keeps source audio, and writes output to the chosen directory.

output_examples.input: A user provides a target character image and a dance reference clip.
output_examples.output: The assistant selects Kling 2.6 Pro Motion Control and structures the request around motion transfer and character identity hold.

output_examples.input: A user wants a quick outfit change on a short portrait video.
output_examples.output: The assistant selects Lucy Edit Restyle and limits the prompt to one localized change with face and motion preservation.

best_practices: State preservation goals before the requested visual change.
Use one edit direction per call to reduce temporal drift.
Confirm media URLs, duration, resolution, and output directory before running the CLI.
anti_patterns: Do not combine background, outfit, product, and lighting changes in one prompt.
Do not submit private media unless third-party RunComfy processing is acceptable.
Do not use reference images unless the edit requires direct visual transfer.

faq.question: Does this skill edit videos locally?
faq.answer: No. It uses the local RunComfy CLI to submit jobs to RunComfy model services.

faq.question: Which model does it use by default?
faq.answer: It defaults to Wan 2.7 Edit-Video when the user does not name a specific route.

faq.question: Can it preserve original audio?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documents audio options for supported routes, including preserving source audio when available.

faq.question: Can it use reference images?
faq.answer: Yes. Reference images are recommended for direct design or appearance transfer, such as packaging changes.

faq.question: What media limits apply?
faq.answer: Limits vary by model. The skill documents duration, resolution, size, and aspect ratio limits for each route.

faq.question: Is it safe for sensitive footage?
faq.answer: Use caution. Prompts and media URLs are sent to RunComfy, so private footage needs an approved data handling process.
